title: Find the largest element in an array
description: Given an array of integers, find the largest element in the array.
driver_python:
# USER_CODE_HERE
import sys
import json
if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    data = json.loads(input_data)
    nums = data['nums']
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.findLargest(nums)
    print(result)

title: Find the second largest element
description: Given an array of integers, find the second largest element in the array. If there is no second largest element, return -1.

    result = solution.findSecondLargest(nums)
    print(result)

title: Two Sum - Find two numbers with target sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
driver_python:
# USER_CODE_HERE
import sys
import json
if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    data = json.loads(input_data)
    nums = data['nums']
    target = data['target']
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.twoSum(nums, target)
    print(json.dumps(result))

title: Rotate array by K positions
description: Given an array, rotate the array to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative.
driver_python:
# USER_CODE_HERE
import sys
import json
if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    data = json.loads(input_data)
    nums = data['nums']
    k = data['k']
    solution = Solution()
    solution.rotate(nums, k)
    print(json.dumps(nums))

title: Find all subarrays with sum = K
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of subarrays whose sum equals to k.
driver_python:
# USER_CODE_HERE
import sys
import json
if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    data = json.loads(input_data)
    nums = data['nums']
    k = data['k']
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.subarraySum(nums, k)
    print(result)

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: You are given an array of integers nums, and an integer k. There is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Return the max sliding window.
driver_python:
# USER_CODE_HERE
import sys
import json
if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    data = json.loads(input_data)
    nums = data['nums']
    k = data['k']
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.maxSlidingWindow(nums, k)
    print(json.dumps(result))

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).
driver_python:
# USER_CODE_HERE
import sys
import json
if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    data = json.loads(input_data)
    nums1 = data['nums1']
    nums2 = data['nums2']
    solution = Solution()
    result = solution.findMedianSortedArrays(nums1, nums2)
    print(f"{result:.5f}")
